questions.question: A radioactive substance decays according to the formula N(t) = N₀ · (1/2)^(t/12), where t is measured in days. If a sample initially contains 640 grams and decays to 10 grams, how many days have passed?
An investment grows continuously at 5% per year. Write an equation for the value A(t) after t years if the initial investment is $3,000. Then calculate how long it takes to double.
questions.explanation: Continuous growth uses A(t) = P·e^(rt). To double: 6000 = 3000e^(0.05t), so 2 = e^(0.05t). Take ln of both sides: ln(2) = 0.05t, so t = ln(2)/0.05 ≈ 13.86 years.

questions.question: A bacterial culture triples every 4 hours. If the initial population is 200 bacteria, after how many hours will the population first exceed 14,400?
A quantity decreases by 8% per month. After how many complete months will the quantity first drop below 30% of its original value?
questions.explanation: Quantity is Q(t) = Q₀ · (0.92)^t. We need 0.92^t < 0.30. Taking ln: t · ln(0.92) < ln(0.30), so t > ln(0.30)/ln(0.92) ≈ 15.1. The first complete month where it drops below 30% is month 16.

questions.question: A loan of $10,000 accrues compound interest at 6% annually. If the total amount owed after n years is $15,000, what is n? (Round to the nearest tenth.)
questions.explanation: Use A = P(1 + r)^n: 15,000 = 10,000(1.06)^n, so 1.5 = 1.06^n. Taking ln: n = ln(1.5)/ln(1.06) ≈ 6.96 years, which rounds to 6.9 or 7.0 depending on rounding convention.

questions.question: The half-life of a substance is 8 years. If you begin with 2 grams, express the amount remaining after t years as a function A(t), then find the amount after 20 years.
